job_description:
Position: Associate Producer, Power Lunch
Location: Englewood Cliffs

Job Description

CNBC is looking for an Associate Producer for Power Lunch. In this role, you will be an integral part of covering the most significant financial, corporate & political news stories of the day.
Responsibilities
• Working with Executive Producer, Senior Producers, Producers and segment producers to produce CNBC Business News program
• Assisting with the coordination of elements for daily business program
• Operating in a high-intensity, fast-paced control room
• Using equipment to play out all on‑air real‑time financial charts
• Writing copy for daily segments
• Being resourceful and creative in helping to deliver the program’s message, including use of clips and graphics
• Researching segment ideas and segment information as directed
• Assisting in the booking of guests
• Assisting producers with story production and pre‑interviews
Benefits

This position is eligible for company‑sponsored benefits, including medical, dental, and vision insurance, 401(k), paid leave, tuition reimbursement, and a variety of other discounts and perks. Salary range: $65,000-$80,000.
Qualifications
• Bachelor’s degree in journalism or business; or an equivalent degree
• At least 2 years of television production experience
• Must be willing and able to work flexible hours, under tight deadline pressure, including breaking news. Must be able to work early morning shift or later day shift.
Desired Characteristics
• Ability to multitask and prioritize assignments, while working with multiple producers and editors on the broadcast and digital teams
• Knowledge of current events, politics and pop culture
• Exemplary interpersonal skills
• Proactive thinker / self‑starter
Equal Employment Opportunity and Additional Information

VERSANT Media’s policy is to provide equal employment opportunities to all applicants and employees without regard to race, color, religion, creed, gender, gender identity or expression, age, national origin or ancestry, citizenship, disability, sexual orientation, marital status, pregnancy, veteran status, membership in the uniformed services, genetic information, or any other basis protected by applicable law.

If you are a qualified individual with a disability or a disabled veteran and require support throughout the application and/or recruitment process as a result of your disability, you have the right to request a reasonable accommodation. You can submit your request to candida tea
.

VERSANT Media is committed to fair and equitable compensation practices. We include a good faith pay range for each position to comply with applicable state and local pay transparency laws and to promote equity across our organization. Actual compensation will be based on factors such as the candidate’s skills, qualifications, experience, and location and may include additional forms of compensation and benefits such as health insurance, retirement plans, paid time off, etc.

VERSANT Media is not accepting unsolicited assistance from search firms for this employment opportunity. All resumes submitted by search firms to any employee at VERSANT via‑email, the Internet, or in any form and/or method without a valid written Statement of Work in place for this position from VERSANT’s Talent Acquisition team will be deemed the sole property of VERSANT. No fee will be paid in the event the candidate is hired by VERSANT as a result of the referral or through other means.
#J-18808-Ljbffr

Qualifications: Bachelor’s degree in journalism or business; or an equivalent degree
At least 2 years of television production experience
Must be willing and able to work flexible hours, under tight deadline pressure, including breaking news
Must be able to work early morning shift or later day shift
Benefits: This position is eligible for company‑sponsored benefits, including medical, dental, and vision insurance, 401(k), paid leave, tuition reimbursement, and a variety of other discounts and perks
We include a good faith pay range for each position to comply with applicable state and local pay transparency laws and to promote equity across our organization

Actual compensation will be based on factors such as the candidate’s skills, qualifications, experience, and location and may include additional forms of compensation and benefits such as health insurance, retirement plans, paid time off, etc

Working with Executive Producer, Senior Producers, Producers and segment producers to produce CNBC Business News program
Assisting with the coordination of elements for daily business program

job_description:
Searching for cast and crew members for "Maybe We Were Fire." a romantic comedy-drama, feature film set in New York, Long Island.

Logline: Danny and Eve first met when they were 18 and fell madly in love. They married young at the end of their senior year of high school and said “I do” fast. Now in their mid-twenties, the spark they once had – their “fire” has run out and they are divorcing. The once inseparable pair finds themselves at a crossroads as they both must navigate what it means to move on from the person you loved the most.

Responsibilities: Logline: Danny and Eve first met when they were 18 and fell madly in love
They married young at the end of their senior year of high school and said “I do” fast

job_description:
At Audible, we believe stories have the power to transform lives. It’s why we work with some of the world’s leading creators to produce and share audio storytelling with our millions of global listeners. We are dreamers and inventors who come from a wide range of backgrounds and experiences to empower and inspire each other. Imagine your future with us.

ABOUT THIS ROLE
The Director of Business Affairs will play a pivotal role in shaping the negotiation strategy and managing the business relationships for the audio Entertainment Originals portfolio. In this role you will collaborate with creative executives and top tier partners in audio, TV, film, music and theater as they work to deliver Audible customers high-quality original content for adaptation on the air, screen and stage. You will report into to the Head of Business Affairs, Entertainment Originals.

ABOUT YOU
You are a valued strategic partner with a history of structuring and closing deals in the entertainment and media industries and are ready to assist the global Audible team in negotiating terms that benefit Audible and delights its customers.

As a Director of Business Affairs, you will...
- Serve as lead negotiator on deals for creators, producers and rights-holders of original audio content for the development, acquisition, and production of entertainment originals
- Evaluate, structure, negotiate, and implement a wide range of agreements independently, while partnering with legal to finalize the full long-form agreements
- Define strategies, expectations, and priorities to ensure team actions align with company success
- Partner with leadership and legal teams to identify and address upfront possible business and legal risks associated with proposed productions, while championing best practices
- Collaborate with internal teams and leadership on the development of strategies for new content initiatives
- Manage, prioritize, and execute high volume deal flow against specific metrics
- Track and communicate deal terms within the organization and provide for first line of enforcement of deal terms, if necessary
- Grow and maintain strong relationships with existing and potential entertainment industry partners such as film/TV studios, celebrities, production studios, writers, agents, managers, and publishers
- Build relationships and represent the entertainment deals’ team to various internal stakeholders including Editorial, Creative, Finance, BI, Marketing, PR, Content Partnerships, and Content Operations teams.
- Work cross-functionally across Audible and other Amazon affiliates (e.g., Amazon Music, Amazon Publishing, etc.) to execute against complex deals and partnership opportunities
- Identify and prepare projects for internal support and valuation by presenting data, preparing budgets, P&Ls, and drafting proposed deal terms - Passionately work on the evolution and consumption of audio content and storytelling

ABOUT AUDIBLE
Audible is the leading producer and provider of audio storytelling. We spark listeners’ imaginations, offering immersive, cinematic experiences full of inspiration and insight to enrich our customers daily lives. We are a global company with an entrepreneurial spirit. We are dreamers and inventors who are passionate about the positive impact Audible can make for our customers and our neighbors. This spirit courses throughout Audible, supporting a culture of creativity and inclusion built on our People Principles and our mission to build more equitable communities in the cities we call home.
Explore the opportunity of Associate Director of Marketing in Red Bank, NJ, USA! Get insights into job responsibilities. Check your eligibility and apply for the position today on Daybook.

At Audible, we believe stories have the power to transform lives.
It's why we work with some of the world's leading creators to produce and share audio storytelling with our millions of global listeners.
We are dreamers and inventors who come from a wide range of backgrounds and experiences to empower and inspire each other.
Imagine your future with us.

ABOUT THIS ROLE
We're seeking a Head of Production, North America to lead Audible's in-house audiovisual production teams across the U.
S.
and Canada, including for audiobooks, audio dramas, podcasts, video content, and live production.
Reporting to the Head of Audible Content, North America, you will execute and oversee all types of production from end-to-end.
As the Head of Production, North America, you will work closely with the Heads of Creative Development and Production Management & Operations to establish and elevate the quality bar for all Audible-produced content, including Audible Originals and Audible Books.
You have a strong creative acumen, financial stewardship, senior management experience, and the ability to implement processes efficiently and sustainably without sacrificing creative excellence or intent.
You balance creative ambition, operational rigor, and strong leadership thriving at the intersection of storytelling, business strategy, and scalable systems.

As the Head of Production, North America, you will.
• Lead production execution, financial oversight, and strategic planning for the studio and production teams
• Foster a culture rooted in collaboration, creative excellence, inclusivity, and efficiency
• Serve as Audible's senior production executive to internal stakeholders and the external creative community
• Lead senior producers, sound designers, directors, and video producers across North America to deliver a high-volume, high-quality slate of Audible Originals, Audible Books, podcasts, and other content types that meet Audible's audience, creative, and business objectives
• Work closely with the Head of Production & Operations to oversee the overall studio budget, P&L, and vendor strategy, optimizing for creative quality, fiscal accountability, and scalability
• Inspire the team to embrace new ways to tell stories in audio-first formats and new technologies, including AI-driven tools
• Build and maintain strong relationships with internal and external creators and production partners across entertainment, publishing, and podcasting

ABOUT AUDIBLE
Audible is the leading producer and provider of audio storytelling.
We spark listeners' imaginations, offering immersive, cinematic experiences full of inspiration and insight to enrich our customers daily lives.
We are a global company with an entrepreneurial spirit.
We are dreamers and inventors who are passionate about the positive impact Audible can make for our customers and our neighbors.
This spirit courses throughout Audible, supporting a culture of creativity and inclusion built on our People Principles and our mission to build more equitable communities in the cities we call home.

Qualifications: You have a strong creative acumen, financial stewardship, senior management experience, and the ability to implement processes efficiently and sustainably without sacrificing creative excellence or intent
You balance creative ambition, operational rigor, and strong leadership thriving at the intersection of storytelling, business strategy, and scalable systems
Responsibilities: and Canada, including for audiobooks, audio dramas, podcasts, video content, and live production
Reporting to the Head of Audible Content, North America, you will execute and oversee all types of production from end-to-end

As the Head of Production, North America, you will work closely with the Heads of Creative Development and Production Management & Operations to establish and elevate the quality bar for all Audible-produced content, including Audible Originals and Audible Books
As the Head of Production, North America, you will
Lead production execution, financial oversight, and strategic planning for the studio and production teams
Foster a culture rooted in collaboration, creative excellence, inclusivity, and efficiency
Serve as Audible's senior production executive to internal stakeholders and the external creative community

Lead senior producers, sound designers, directors, and video producers across North America to deliver a high-volume, high-quality slate of Audible Originals, Audible Books, podcasts, and other content types that meet Audible's audience, creative, and business objectives
Work closely with the Head of Production & Operations to oversee the overall studio budget, P&L, and vendor strategy, optimizing for creative quality, fiscal accountability, and scalability
Inspire the team to embrace new ways to tell stories in audio-first formats and new technologies, including AI-driven tools
Build and maintain strong relationships with internal and external creators and production partners across entertainment, publishing, and podcasting

job_description:
At Audible, we believe stories have the power to transform lives. It’s why we work with some of the world’s leading creators to produce and share audio storytelling with our millions of global listeners. We are dreamers and inventors who come from a wide range of backgrounds and experiences to empower and inspire each other. Imagine your future with us.

ABOUT THIS ROLE
The Associate Director, Production and Operations will play a critical role in building and optimizing the production systems that enable Audible Studios to deliver premium content. This position serves as a strategic operational leader who orchestrates seamless collaboration between cross functional teams and workflows to ensure excellence at every stage of the content lifecycle—from conception to delivery.
This role is ideal for a systems thinker and problem-solver who thrives in dynamic and creative environments, balances quality with efficiency, and builds scalable frameworks that empower creative teams to do their best work.

As an Associate Director of Production and Operations, you will…
- Work closely with creative teams to understand audio content production processes and identify opportunities for improvement
- Facilitate clear communication and seamless handoffs across teams to support end-to-end production from concept through final delivery
- Coordinate recurring and ad hoc cross-team meetings that require multi-departmental involvement
- Identify process inefficiencies and develop solutions that improve both quality and efficiency
- Develop standardized production templates, schedules, and documentation frameworks that enable teams to deliver exceptional content
- Systematically capture lessons learned and feed insights back into workflow design to support continuous improvement
- Design and implement scalable production systems and workflows that support current operations and future innovations
- Implement tools and technologies that enhance operational effectiveness
- Train internal teams and stakeholders on system usage and drive adoption of best practices
- Coach and mentor team members to build operational capabilities
- Lead the integration of new tools, processes, and workflows while supporting teams through change
- Partner with leadership and cross-functional teams to communicate changes, address concerns, and build organizational capability

ABOUT AUDIBLE
Audible is the leading producer and provider of audio storytelling. We spark listeners’ imaginations, offering immersive, cinematic experiences full of inspiration and insight to enrich our customers daily lives. We are a global company with an entrepreneurial spirit. We are dreamers and inventors who are passionate about the positive impact Audible can make for our customers and our neighbors. This spirit courses throughout Audible, supporting a culture of creativity and inclusion built on our People Principles and our mission to build more equitable communities in the cities we call home.

THE JOB

The production department steers the execution, management and administration of production operation details on all 50+ events a year. In tandem with operations, talent management, and finance, the department plays a direct role in liaising with clients, event planners, performing talent, venues, technical and site vendors, labor unions and other key planning agencies to ensure success for all events both front and back of house.

In this role, the Production Manager will have the opportunity to manage and support various events throughout the year, creating new ways to address unique challenges and collaborating directly with internal and external stakeholders through the process. The ideal candidate will have the ability to handle multiple projects and adapt to fast-paced changing environments while maintaining quality and exemplary leadership and teamwork.

WHAT THIS ROLE WILL DO

• Be responsible for all the on-stage, backstage, and other technical and site planning details of events

• Develop and maintain relative documents including production and site drawings, on-sale layouts, timelines, schedules and artist technical riders

• Advance and obtain technical information, including venue details, production needs, rider requirements, staging, and event capacity changes

• Ensure all production elements are executed on time, on budget, and to the highest quality standards

• Advance technical and site planning details for assigned shows/events

• Maintain regular communications with all stakeholders

• Assist in managing technical production (i.e. stage, sound, video, lighting, backline, broadcast, labor crews)

• Assist in managing site operations (i.e. power, heavy equipment, traffic management, crowd control measures, sanitation, permitting and engineering)

• Assist with budget management and tracking of assigned responsibilities

• Oversee multiple projects from start to finish, including presence on-site from load in

• Ensure positive and creative environment for planning teams

• Proactively identify and resolve production risks or issues before they impact show timelines or client satisfaction

• Ensure compliance with all relevant health, safety, and labor regulations

• Facilitate proper interdepartmental communication and organization

• Follow up on all action points from internal and external meetings

• Assist in producing event debrief documents including settlement notes for finance and suggested notes for the following year

• Regular liaising with managing director via weekly meetings, calls and email communications

WHAT THIS PERSON WILL BRING

Essential:

• Detailed project management / planning experience

• Production management experience (3+ years)

• Vectorworks knowledge and ability to use / AutoCAD experience acceptable

• Computer literate in Windows applications

• Exceptional organizational and administrative skills, both written and verbal

• Ability to prioritize and meet deadlines

• Procurement of vendors (staging, audio, lighting, video…etc) and managing staff, services, and budgets

• Working under pressure and ability to make clear concise decisions, sometimes with limited information

• Ability to work on and manage multiple projects simultaneously

• Flexible work and extensive travel schedule (days/nights, late hours, weekends, and holidays)

• Strong people skills with an emphasis on competent and diplomatic communication with client, vendor and artist representatives

• Ability to learn and adapt to new technology, information, resources and environments

• Tolerance of all cultures, music and art forms

• Tolerance of loud noise level in working environment

Desirable:

• Experience planning large scale events (5,000 people +)

• Experience in stage management / show running

• Experience in site planning and coordination

• Negotiation and liaising with agencies

• Event management training

• Health and safety/crowd management training

• Excellent communication, collaboration and analytical skills

• Exceptional attention to detail and accuracy

• Exceptional organizational and administrative skills

• Anticipatory and proactive approach to problem solving Reference: ECNJOBID-66-249-70-98 in the application

Overview

This is a Full Time position in the field of Production Coordinator / Project Management
, located in New York, NY
.

Versant is a new, independent media company formed from Comcast NBCUniversal, uniting leading cable television networks including USA Network, CNBC, MSNBC, Oxygen, E!, SYFY, and Golf Channel, along with premier digital properties such as Fandango, Rotten Tomatoes, Golf Now, Golf Pass, and Sports Engine. Anchored by news, sports, and entertainment, Versant is built for the future of media—agile, digital-first, and driven by innovation.
Position

Versant Media is seeking an accomplished Vice President, Production Operations to oversee day-to-day technical and studio operations across our major production facilities in New York, New Jersey, Washington D.C., and Los Angeles. This leader will manage core live production functions, ensure operational excellence, and drive innovation in partnership with cross-functional teams. The ideal candidate combines deep technical knowledge, exceptional organizational skills, and a forward-looking mindset to improve quality, efficiency, and scalability in production workflows.
Responsibilities
• Oversee control rooms and studio operations across NY, NJ, Washington D.C., and Los Angeles
• Manage technical operations functions for live production across news, sports, and entertainment
• Partner closely with post production, field operations, and media operations teams to ensure seamless execution of live and pre-recorded shows
• Collaborate with engineering to ensure consistently reliable production systems and rapid issue resolution
• Optimize schedules to maximize efficiency in crew deployment and production resource utilization
• Drive adoption of new production technologies and workflows to enhance quality, reduce marginal costs, and enable new creative opportunities
• Support multi-platform production, including linear TV, streaming, digital, and audio formats
• Foster a culture of operational excellence, collaboration, and continuous improvement across production teams
Experience

Qualifications
• 10+ years of experience in production operations, including live studio or control room environments
• Proven experience managing multi-location technical operations teams and facilities
• Strong knowledge of live broadcast and digital production systems, workflows, and technology trends
• Track record of optimizing operational efficiency through scheduling, process improvements, and technology adoption
• Collaborative leadership style with the ability to build strong partnerships across editorial, engineering, and operations teams
• Excellent organizational, problem-solving, and communication skills
• Ability to operate in a fast-paced, multi-stakeholder environment while maintaining high production standards
• Onsite:
This position has been designated as onsite, meaning that the position is expected to contribute from a Versant worksite (NYC or Englewood Cliffs, NJ).

We have partnered with Production

HUB to provide you with the most valuable and exclusive job service in the industry.

job_description:
# Executive Producer, Game Presentation & Entertainment## •### @GothamFCPosted on 12/4/2025•Full-time## Job Description**The Role:**Gotham FC is seeking an Executive Producer of Game Presentation & Entertainment who will lead and enhance the overall fan experience for Gotham FC matches, ensuring every aspect of gameday contributes to fan engagement and satisfaction.You will lead and produce Gotham FC’s full-season game presentation strategy, from pre-match through post-match, aligning with theme nights, club traditions, partner activations, and ticketing initiatives. You’ll be responsible for elevating the fan experience through engaging storytelling, live entertainment, and seamless production execution. This role is both creative and operational, requiring strong leadership in live event production, cross-departmental collaboration, and control room management. This position reports to the VP of Brand and Fan Experience.**Key Responsibilities:****Game Presentation Leadership*** Own the development and execution of Gotham FC’s matchday experience across all elements: pregame, in-match, halftime, and postgame.* Build and maintain a season-long game presentation plan that integrates theme nights, group experiences, promotions, and evolving fan traditions.* Call and direct all home matches, commanding control room operations and ensuring cohesive delivery across lighting, sound, video, graphics, and live entertainment.* Hire, train, schedule, and lead a team of field producers responsible for managing on-field activities, live promotions, and in-match entertainment.* Develop staffing plans and run-of-show assignments that align with match programming and operational needs.* Manage, source and mentor in-stadium talent (in-stadium DJ, in-match host, PA Announcer), hype crew, and control room staff ensuring high standards of execution and communication.* Serve as the direct manager for the Junior Graphic Designer, providing creative feedback, prioritizing workload, and supporting professional development to align with the broader fan experience vision.**Planning & Coordination*** Write and manage detailed game scripts (via ShoFlo), including creative copy for talent and cueing across departments (PA, music, lighting, video, performance, partnerships).* Lead weekly match production meetings and serve as the central point of coordination across internal departments and external vendors.* Collaborate with Partnership Marketing, Ticketing, and Marketing teams to integrate sponsor activations, promotions, and fan experiences into matchday plans.* Oversee timelines and delivery of all production assets (graphics, videos, sponsor elements) via shared platforms (Box, Google Drive, etc.).* Partner with venue control room staff to test, load, and troubleshoot all technical content ahead of matchdays.* Work closely with the Operations team to align on logistics for all in-match entertainment, ensuring compliance with stadium requirements, safety protocols, and timelines.* Secure participants and VIP talent for pregame traditions, on-field recognitions or special presentations.* Collaborate with Partnership Marketing to ensure all sponsor deliverables are fulfilled and executed to brand standards, while developing new concepts and creative opportunities for partners that enhance fan engagement and add value to the in-stadium experience.* Coordinate with the NWSL and other league representatives to ensure compliance and alignment with all league-wide initiatives, brand campaigns, and match presentation guidelines.* Manage communication and logistics for VIP guests, honorees, and special talent appearances to ensure a seamless and professional matchday experience.* Source and manage entertainment vendors and special programming (e.g., National Anthem performers, halftime talent, pyro, performers).* Collaborate with the Design team to concept and deliver all in-stadium graphics, ensuring visual consistency and alignment with club branding and themes.* Collaborate closely with the Ticketing team to plan and execute Fan Experience Packages (FEPs) and group experiences, including on-field performances, pregame ceremonies, on-field parades, and other presentations.\*\*Fan Experience & Innovation \*\*Oversee fan engagement elements such as contests, trivia, and interactive videoboard features that enhance the live experience.* Monitor fan feedback and matchday analytics to evolve and innovate programming.* Develop scalable and engaging fan and group experiences that drive sales while enhancing the overall matchday atmosphere.* Identify new opportunities to create memorable fan moments that strengthen community connection and contribute to ticketing and retention goals.* Support the development of new traditions and moments that strengthen the Gotham FC brand and community connection.* Build strong relationships with Gotham FC supporter groups and fan collectives to foster collaboration, enhance matchday atmosphere, and create shared traditions that celebrate club culture**Qualifications:*** Bachelor’s Degree in Marketing, Communications, Production, or related field, or 5+ years of relevant live event/game presentation experience.* Proven experience calling and producing live sports events in a control room environment.* Strong understanding of sponsorship integration, ticketing-driven activations, and multi-brand collaborations.* Proficiency in ShoFlo (cloud-based event scripting and show-calling software) or similar production management platforms.* Exceptional organizational and project management skills; ability to manage multiple concurrent events and deadlines.* Outstanding written and verbal communication skills.* Strong leadership and interpersonal skills with experience managing staff and cross-functional teams.* Working knowledge of Adobe Creative Suite (Photoshop, Illustrator, etc.) and the ability to provide creative direction and feedback on visual content.* Demonstrated creativity and innovation in live entertainment or sports presentation.* Ability to make quick, sound decisions in a fast-paced, high-pressure environment.* Willingness to work evenings, weekends, holidays, and extended hours as required by match schedule.**Capability & Other Characteristics:*** Experience in professional soccer or familiarity with global soccer fan culture.* Background in game presentation, entertainment marketing, fan engagement, or live event production.* Strategic thinker with strong attention to detail.* Calm under pressure, collaborative, and solutions-oriented.* Legally authorized to work in the U.S.**Location:**Based at Gotham FC’s business office, which is located at 15 Exchange Place Jersey City, New Jersey, on a hybrid working schedule which consists of three core in-office/in-person days (Tuesday, Wednesday, and Thursday) and two non-core days (Monday and Friday), with the option of being in the business office or remote. Ability to attend all home games and any mandatory events as well as away games, as necessary. A willingness to travel for other business-related needs, as they arise. Ability to work at the team’s soccer operations office in Whippany, New Jersey, as needed. Gotham FC requires its associates to live in its market, that is, within reasonable commuting distance of Gotham FC’s offices, practice facility and Sports Illustrated Stadium.\*\*\*\*Gotham FC is an equal opportunity employer and welcomes everyone to our team. We are committed to broadening the pool of qualified talent for consideration, including those who have been historically underrepresented. We strongly encourage people of color, lesbian, gay, bisexual, transgender, queer and non-binary people, veterans, parents, and individuals with disabilities to apply.**Job type:** Full-time Exempt position**Salary range:** $80,000 - $100,000 annually**Benefits**: Medical, Dental & Vision benefit plans available
#J-18808-Ljbffr

About Kinetic Brands

Unconventional brands for unconventional people.

Kinetic is a full-stack brand incubator owning a portfolio of digital brands. The brands we build are the opposite of basic. Each brand has a distinct identity, memorable product, and cult following. Through our full-stack infrastructure (meaning we manage everything in-house–including fulfillment, supply chain, R&D, marketing, and development), we can go first to market with any brand concept and create something truly bespoke that stands out even in the most crowded of industries.

About the Role

At Kinetic Brands, we are passionate about creating exceptional products and experiences that resonate with our community. We are seeking a talented Content Producer to join our in-house creative team and own the execution of monthly product launch deliverables. This role is hybrid, with weekly photoshoots and creative collaboration in our New Jersey office.

In this role, you will lead the execution of content across photo, video, design, and performance marketing, owning video editing and storytelling for high-impact campaigns while ensuring all assets meet a high standard of quality and brand consistency. You will contribute to creative concepting for launches and paid media, translating strategic direction into effective execution in partnership with the Creative and Art Director, while also guiding content producers and freelancers through feedback and QA. You’ll support photoshoots from planning through on-set execution, including second shooting, and drive performance marketing creative through ongoing testing and iteration. With a strong focus on efficiency, you will continuously improve workflows, file organization, and production processes to elevate both output and team performance.

If you’re ambitious, detail-oriented, and excited to contribute to a fast-moving creative team within an established and passionate community, we’d love to hear from you.

You will
Lead execution of content across photo, video, design, and performance marketing Own video editing and storytelling for high-impact campaigns and ads Oversee and elevate retouching, design, and final asset quality Concept and contribute creative ideas for launches, ads, and campaigns Partner closely with Creative/Art Director to translate strategy into execution Guide content producers and freelancers (feedback, direction, QA) Support photoshoots with planning + second shooting (photo/video) Drive performance marketing creative (Meta ads, testing variations, iteration) Improve workflows, file organization, and production efficiency

You have
BA or equivalent in relevant field 5+ years of relevant experience in content creation, creative production, or digital marketing Experience working in an e-commerce or direct-to-consumer environment A strong portfolio demonstrating high-quality visual execution, with an emphasis on video, storytelling, and brand consistency Excellent time management and prioritization skills with the ability to manage multiple projects and deadlines simultaneously Strong collaboration skills and the ability to work cross-functionally with creative and marketing teams Experience leading or guiding creative work, including providing feedback and direction to team members or freelancers Hands-on experience with Shopify, including product listing management and merchandising Advanced proficiency in Adobe Creative Suite and Google Workspace, with strong video editing capabilities Experience developing content for performance marketing channels (Meta, TikTok, paid social) Experience supporting or leading photoshoots, including photography and videography (second shooting or capture experience) Exceptional attention to detail and a strong eye for visual quality and brand execution A proactive, solution-oriented mindset with the ability to take ownership, give direction, and continuously improve

Bonus points
Strong awareness of internet culture, social media trends, and digital communities UX/UI experience (desktop and mobile)

Perks
Healthcare You Can Count On – Competitive medical, dental, and vision plans to keep you covered when it matters most. Unlimited PTO – Take the time you need—we trust you (plus, we require a minimum!). Paid Parental Leave – 12 weeks paid + 4 weeks eased return to work. Family first! 401k Match – We match 4% to help grow your future. Monthly Home Office Stipend – We cover part of your phone or internet bill. Employee Discounts – Big savings on our products, just for you!

Work environment
Hybrid: This role requires on-site work at our Passaic, NJ office 2-3 days a week.

Kinetic Brands is an equal opportunity employer — we believe being successful is directly tied to creating a collaborative, diverse, and inclusive environment.

A company is looking for a Senior Producer, Live Content. Key Responsibilities Own production delivery for live content initiatives including seasonal content and collaborations Drive execution from concept through release validation in partnership with various stakeholders Identify production risks early and lead mitigation strategies across cross-functional teams Required Qualifications 5+ years of hands-on production experience in game development or live service environments Demonstrated ownership of delivery for complex content releases or multi-team initiatives Experience shipping updates on a live game with active player impact Proven experience working with agile production workflows and iterative development environments Advanced experience with Jira and Confluence as production infrastructure

About Kinetic Brands

Unconventional brands for unconventional people.

Kinetic is a full-stack brand incubator owning a portfolio of digital brands. The brands we build are the opposite of basic. Each brand has a distinct identity, memorable product, and cult following. Through our full-stack infrastructure (meaning we manage everything in-house–including fulfillment, supply chain, R&D, marketing, and development), we can go first to market with any brand concept and create something truly bespoke that stands out even in the most crowded of industries.

About the Role

At Kinetic Brands, we are passionate about creating exceptional products and experiences that resonate with our community. We are seeking a talented Content Producer to join our in-house creative team and own the execution of monthly product launch deliverables. This role is hybrid, with weekly photoshoots and creative collaboration in our New Jersey office.

In this role, you will lead the execution of content across photo, video, design, and performance marketing, owning video editing and storytelling for high-impact campaigns while ensuring all assets meet a high standard of quality and brand consistency. You will contribute to creative concepting for launches and paid media, translating strategic direction into effective execution in partnership with the Creative and Art Director, while also guiding content producers and freelancers through feedback and QA. You’ll support photoshoots from planning through on-set execution, including second shooting, and drive performance marketing creative through ongoing testing and iteration. With a strong focus on efficiency, you will continuously improve workflows, file organization, and production processes to elevate both output and team performance.

If you’re ambitious, detail-oriented, and excited to contribute to a fast-moving creative team within an established and passionate community, we’d love to hear from you.

You will
• Lead execution of content across photo, video, design, and performance marketing
• Own video editing and storytelling for high-impact campaigns and ads
• Oversee and elevate retouching, design, and final asset quality
• Concept and contribute creative ideas for launches, ads, and campaigns
• Partner closely with Creative/Art Director to translate strategy into execution
• Guide content producers and freelancers (feedback, direction, QA)
• Support photoshoots with planning + second shooting (photo/video)
• Drive performance marketing creative (Meta ads, testing variations, iteration)
• Improve workflows, file organization, and production efficiency

You have
• BA or equivalent in relevant field
• 5+ years of relevant experience in content creation, creative production, or digital marketing
• Experience working in an e-commerce or direct-to-consumer environment
• A strong portfolio demonstrating high-quality visual execution, with an emphasis on video, storytelling, and brand consistency
• Excellent time management and prioritization skills with the ability to manage multiple projects and deadlines simultaneously
• Strong collaboration skills and the ability to work cross-functionally with creative and marketing teams
• Experience leading or guiding creative work, including providing feedback and direction to team members or freelancers
• Hands-on experience with Shopify, including product listing management and merchandising
• Advanced proficiency in Adobe Creative Suite and Google Workspace, with strong video editing capabilities
• Experience developing content for performance marketing channels (Meta, TikTok, paid social)
• Experience supporting or leading photoshoots, including photography and videography (second shooting or capture experience)
• Exceptional attention to detail and a strong eye for visual quality and brand execution
• A proactive, solution-oriented mindset with the ability to take ownership, give direction, and continuously improve

Bonus points
• Strong awareness of internet culture, social media trends, and digital communities
• UX/UI experience (desktop and mobile)

Perks
• Healthcare You Can Count On – Competitive medical, dental, and vision plans to keep you covered when it matters most.
• Unlimited PTO – Take the time you need—we trust you (plus, we require a minimum!).
• Paid Parental Leave – 12 weeks paid + 4 weeks eased return to work. Family first!
• 401k Match – We match 4% to help grow your future.
• Monthly Home Office Stipend – We cover part of your phone or internet bill.
• Employee Discounts – Big savings on our products, just for you!

Work environment
• Hybrid: This role requires on-site work at our Passaic, NJ office 2-3 days a week.

Kinetic Brands is an equal opportunity employer — we believe being successful is directly tied to creating a collaborative, diverse, and inclusive environment.

job_title: National Brand Anchor & Senior Content Producer (Tech & Business Services/AiCX)
job_description: Overview Title: National Brand Anchor & Senior Content Producer Compensation $120,000 + Up to $30,000 of Performance Incentives (Travel up to 25%) We are seeking a full-time fully dedicated dynamic and experienced Brand Anchor & Senior Content Producer (Tech & Business Services/AiCX) to serve as the on-camera face and voice of our company across short-to-medium form post and video content and as a multimedia specialist (MMS). This hybrid role blends the professionalism and presence of a corporate news anchor with hands-on content creation and production skills. You will research, script, produce, host, and optimize engaging video content for social media platforms (LinkedIn, YouTube Shorts, TikTok, Instagram Reels, X, etc.) and paid advertising campaigns. Your content will focus on CEO/Founder Brand, Executive Leadership, tech innovations, business and technology products and services, industry insights, thought leadership, product explanations, customer stories, and company updates — all while maintaining a polished, credible, and authoritative delivery style. This is an ideal opportunity for a former news anchor, broadcast journalist, or dynamic digital host looking to leverage their acumen, skills, and capabilities to play a meaningful role in corporate brand storytelling. Remote Work-at-Home Top-tier Top-tier (Former network/local anchor/or significant digital media host e.g. with proven social video results) Compensation $120,000 + Up to $30,000 of Performance Incentives Total Compensation Package (Beyond Base Salary) Monthly Performance Incentives: Up to $2,500 of base (tied to content performance metrics like engagement, leads, or ad ROI). Benefits: Work From Home (Up to 75%) Health, dental, vision insurance 401(k) Flexible PTO Professional development (courses, equipment stipends) Home office or production gear allowance Other Perks (common in this role): Equipment budget (camera, lighting, microphone, editing software licenses) Travel for shoots or events (Travel up to 25%) Position Responsibilities On-Camera Hosting & Anchoring: Serve as the primary on-camera talent and brand anchor, delivering professional, confident, and engaging presentations in short-to-medium form videos (15 seconds to 5 minutes). Host explainer videos, industry commentary, executive interviews, product/service demos, thought leadership pieces, and promotional content. Maintain a consistent, trustworthy on-screen presence that reflects the company’s professional image. Content Ideation & Strategy: Research and brainstorm compelling content ideas aligned with company goals, marketing campaigns, product launches, and industry trends in tech and business services. Develop content calendars and series concepts tailored for social media and paid advertising performance. Collaborate with marketing, sales, product, and leadership teams to identify key messages and storytelling opportunities. Scriptwriting & Pre-Production: Write clear, concise, and engaging scripts optimized for video delivery and audience retention. Conduct background research, gather facts, prepare talking points, and develop interview questions when featuring internal experts or clients. Plan shoots, including shot lists, locations, props, and visual elements. Full-Cycle Video Production: Produce high-quality short-to-medium form videos from concept to final delivery. Shoot, direct, and self-host video content (solo or with crew support). Oversee or perform lighting, audio, framing, and on-location filming as needed. Edit videos using tools like Adobe Premiere, Final Cut, CapCut, or similar — including cuts, graphics, text overlays, music, sound design, and basic motion graphics. Optimize videos for different platforms (aspect ratios, captions, hooks, CTAs, etc.). Paid Advertising & Social Media Optimization: Create digital, post, and video assets specifically designed for paid social campaigns (Meta, LinkedIn Ads, YouTube, etc.), ensuring strong hooks, clear messaging, and high conversion potential. Adapt and repurpose longer content into platform-specific short-form versions. Incorporate SEO best practices, trending audio, captions, and hashtags for organic reach. Performance Analysis & Iteration: Monitor content performance metrics (views, engagement, click-through rates, conversions) across platforms. Analyze audience feedback and data to refine future content and improve results. Provide regular reports and recommendations to the marketing team. Brand Representation & Compliance: Act as a professional spokesperson and ambassador for the company and/or Founder/CEO in all content. Ensure all videos maintain brand voice, tone, visual standards, and messaging guidelines. Stay up-to-date with platform algorithms, content trends, and best practices in tech/business storytelling. Collaboration & Additional Duties: Work closely with Founder/CEO, marketers and executives to support broader campaigns. Participate in live streams, webinars, or events when required. Manage or coordinate with freelance editors, videographers, or agencies for periodic larger scale projects. Contribute to overall content strategy and help evolve the Founder/CEO’s and company’s social media and video presence. Paid advertising focus: Experience optimizing posts and videos for X, Meta, LinkedIn Ads, or YouTube to positively impact company ROI. Candidate Qualifications Experience 3–7+ years as a corporate news anchor, broadcast journalist, TV host, or equivalent on-camera role. Proven experience producing, scripting, shooting, and editing short-to-medium form video content for social media or digital platforms. Strong portfolio showcasing on-camera work and produced video content (especially in tech, business, or B2B contexts preferred). Skills: Excellent on-camera presence, clear articulation, and natural delivery style. Strong writing and storytelling skills with the ability to simplify complex tech/business topics. Proficiency in video production (shooting + editing) and familiarity with social media content tools. Understanding of paid advertising creative requirements and platform best practices. Knowledge of tech and business services industries is a significant plus. Personal Attributes: Professional demeanor, high attention to detail, and strong work ethic. Ability to work independently as a self-starter while collaborating effectively with cross-functional teams. Creativity balanced with strategic thinking and data-driven decision making. About MCI (Parent Company) MCI helps customers take on their CX and DX challenges differently, creating industry-leading solutions that deliver exceptional experiences and drive optimal performance. MCI assists companies with business process outsourcing, staff augmentation, contact center customer services, and IT Services needs by providing general and specialized hosting, software, staff, and services. In 2019, Marlowe Companies Inc. (MCI) was named by Inc. Magazine as Iowa’s Fastest Growing Company in the State of Iowa and was named the 452nd Fastest Growing Privately Company in the USA, making the coveted top 500 for the first time. MCI’s subsidiaries had previously made Inc. Magazine's List of Fastest-Growing Companies 15 times, respectively. MCI has ten business process outsourcing service delivery facilities in Georgia, Florida, Texas, New Mexico, California, Kansas, Nova Scotia, South Africa, and the Philippines. Driving modernization through digitalization, MCI ensures clients do more for less. MCI is the holding company for a diverse lineup of tech-enabled business services operating companies. MCI organically grows, acquires, and operates companies that have synergistic products and services portfolios, including but not limited to Automated Contact Center Solutions (ACCS), customer contact management, IT Services (IT Schedule 70), and Temporary and Administrative Professional Staffing (TAPS Schedule 736), Business Process Management (BPM), Business Process Outsourcing (BPO), Claims Processing, Collections, Customer Experience Provider (CXP), Customer Service, Digital Experience Provider (DXP), Account Receivables Management (ARM), Application Software Development, Managed Services, and Technology Services, to mid-market, Federal & enterprise partners. MCI now employs 10,000+ talented individuals with 150+ diverse North American client partners across the following MCI brands: MCI BPO, MCI BPOaaS, MarketForce, GravisApps, Gravis Marketing, MarchEast, Mass Markets, MCI Federal Services (MFS), OnBrand24, The Sydney Call Center, Valor Intelligent Processing (VIP), BYC Aqua, EastWest BPO, TeleTechnology, and Vinculum. Qualifications & Requirements Experience 3–7+ years as a corporate news anchor, broadcast journalist, TV host, or equivalent on-camera role. Proven experience producing, scripting, shooting, and editing short-to-medium form video content for social media or digital platforms. Strong portfolio showcasing on-camera work and produced video content (especially in tech, business, or B2B contexts preferred). Skills: Excellent on-camera presence, clear articulation, and natural delivery style. Strong writing and storytelling skills with the ability to simplify complex tech/business topics. Proficiency in video production (shooting + editing) and familiarity with social media content tools. Understanding of paid advertising creative requirements and platform best practices. Knowledge of tech and business services industries is a significant plus. Personal Attributes: Professional demeanor, high attention to detail, and strong work ethic. Ability to work independently as a self-starter while collaborating effectively with cross-functional teams. Creativity balanced with strategic thinking and data-driven decision making. Conditions of Employment All MCI Locations Must be authorized to work in the country where the job is based. Subject to the program and location of the position Must be willing to submit up to a LEVEL II background and/or security investigation with a fingerprint. Job offers are contingent on background/security investigation results. Must be willing to submit to drug screening. Job offers are contingent on drug screening results. (Does not apply in Canada) Physical Requirements This job operates in a professional office environment. While performing the duties of this job, the employee will be largely sedentary and will be required to sit/stand for long periods while using a computer and telephone headset. The employee will be regularly required to operate a computer and other office equipment, including a phone, copier, and printer. The employee may occasionally be required to move about the office to accomplish tasks; reach in any direction; raise or lower objects, move objects from place to place, hold onto objects, and move or exert force up to forty (40) pounds. Reasonable Accommodation Consistent with the Americans with Disabilities Act (ADA), it is the policy of MCI and its affiliates to provide reasonable accommodations when requested by a qualified applicant or employee with a disability unless such accommodations would cause undue hardship. The policy regarding requests for reasonable accommodation applies to all aspects of employment. If reasonable accommodations are needed, please contact Human Resources. Diversity and Equality At MCI and its subsidiaries, we embrace differences and believe diversity is a benefit to our employees, our company, our customers, and our community. All aspects of employment at MCI are based solely on a person's merit and qualifications. MCI maintains a work environment free from discrimination, one where employees are treated with dignity and respect. All employees share in the responsibility for fulfilling MCI's commitment to a diverse and equal opportunity work environment. MCI does not discriminate against any employee or applicant on the basis of age, ancestry, color, family or medical care leave, gender identity or expression, genetic information, marital status, medical condition, national origin, physical or mental disability, political affiliation, protected veteran status, race, religion, sex (including pregnancy), sexual orientation, or any other characteristic protected by applicable laws, regulations, and ordinances. MCI will consider for employment qualified applicants with criminal histories in a manner consistent with local and federal requirements. MCI will not tolerate discrimination or harassment based on any of these characteristics. We adhere to these principles in all aspects of employment, including recruitment, hiring, training, compensation, promotion, benefits, social and recreational programs, and discipline. In addition, it is the policy of MCI to provide reasonable accommodation to qualified employees who have protected disabilities to the extent required by applicable laws, regulations, and ordinances where an employee works. #J-18808-Ljbffr

job_description:
At MJH Life Sciences our success is measured by your success! If you set your standards high and want to contribute to a winning team, we’ll provide you with every opportunity to help grow our company and your career. Our associates come from all backgrounds, sharing one key quality: determination to succeed. We value being Service Focused, having a Passion for Winning, Innovation, Respect, Integrity, and Teamwork.

Nothing means more to us than hiring people with these attributes. If you believe you’re right for the job, this is the place to prove it!

The Senior Content Producer is a core leader on the Content Strategist track, elevating brand authority within a therapeutic area through deep scientific fluency, KOL relationship-building, and premium program development. This role blends content strategy, multimedia production, and on-camera presence to shape high-impact, omnichannel content that clinicians trust.
• * What You’ll Do
• * As a Senior Content Producer at MJH Life Sciences, you’ll own the content direction of your therapeutic area, build long-term partnerships with top-tier experts, and steer flagship content programs from concept through launch. You’ll represent the brand at major medical meetings, guide junior producers, and translate complex scientific developments into engaging video, digital, and social content that informs and influences clinician audiences.
• * Key Responsibilities:
• *** Therapeutic Strategy Leadership
• ** Develop and refine the content roadmap for your assigned therapeutic area, identifying content pillars, unmet educational needs, and opportunities for strategic growth.
• Monitor clinical pipelines, guideline shifts, FDA actions, and emerging data to drive timely, relevant content decisions.
• * Advanced KOL Engagement
• ** Build, manage, and deepen relationships with top-tier KOLs, clinical trial investigators, and society leaders.
• Shape repeatable, scalable content models that leverage ongoing faculty participation and anchor brand credibility.
• * Program Ownership
• ** Concept, script, and lead high-visibility content programs—including premium video series, expert panels, and editorially independent franchise content.
• Ensure scientific accuracy, strong editorial framing, and strategic alignment across all programs.
• Serve as the on-camera host and public-facing representative for recorded and live media appearances.
• Lead on-site congress coverage, coordinating KOL engagements and producing premium multimedia and post-event reporting.
• * Mentorship & Oversight
• ** Coach and review work from Content Producers and Content Associates, providing direction, quality control, and development feedback.
• Oversee discussion guides, video outlines, and digital content to ensure clarity, accuracy, and brand consistency.
• * Omnichannel Stewardship
• ** Oversee the voice, tone, and content mix for your assigned brand’s website, newsletters, and social channels.
• Lead quarterly audits and strategic content planning to ensure a cohesive, clinician-centered experience.
• Identify evergreen and topical content opportunities grounded in therapeutic relevance.
• * Partnerships & Revenue Enablement
• ** Partner with Business Development and Marketing teams to shape strategic pitches and identify white space opportunities.
• Align content excellence with commercial needs while upholding editorial integrity and clinician trust.
• * What Sets You Apart
• ** You’re a scientific storyteller — equally adept at digesting data and creating compelling narratives.
• You’re charismatic, outgoing, and confident on camera, able to build rapport with anyone from world-leading KOLs to early-career clinicians.
• You thrive at medical meetings, forging relationships and capturing insights in real time.
• You balance creativity with strategic thinking, always aligning content with clinician needs and brand goals.
• You enjoy coaching others and elevating the standards of content quality and execution.
• You see the big picture and connect clinical trends to meaningful, multi-format content opportunities.
• * Why MJH Life Sciences
• * MJH Life Sciences is the largest privately held, independent medical media company in North…
